Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC154 focuses on decomposing a requirement and temporary linking but does not reference DOORS or demonstrate management of requirements in a third-party RM tool.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC155 covers refining a requirement and establishing temporary links; it does not mention DOORS or verify that requirements are managed in a 3rd-party RM tool.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC156 describes modifying a requirement and related event messaging but contains no reference to DOORS or verification that requirements are managed in a third-party RM tool.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC158 describes merging requirements and post-conditions for merge behavior but contains no mention of DOORS or third-party requirement management; it does not verify that requirements are managed in DOORS.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: This test explicitly references polling DOORS to obtain requirement-related quantitative information, which shows use of DOORS in runtime behavior. However it does not assert that requirements are stored/managed in DOORS as the authoritative RM tool, nor does it verify CRUD, synchronization, access control, or error handling with DOORS. Therefore it provides partial evidence of the requirement.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC141 mentions establishing a trace between a UML artifact and a requirement (UML artifact is a non-requirement artifact type). However it does not assert or verify that the UML artifact is managed in a third-party tool (Rational Rose/ClearCase) or that management/integration with such tools occurs. No assertion about storage location, tool integration, or management in 3rd party tools is present.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC154 concerns decomposing requirements and sending decompose event messages. It addresses requirement decomposition and temporary links, but does not mention non-requirement artifacts or their management in third-party tools.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC155 describes refining a requirement and establishing temporary links; it does not reference non-requirement artifacts or management in third-party tools such as Rational Rose or ClearCase.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC157 covers deleting a requirement and link status changes; it does not address non-requirement artifacts or management in Rational Rose/ClearCase or other third-party tools.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC162 focuses on constructing composite event messages and polling DOORS for quantitative information. It references linking related requirements and DOORS, but does not reference management of non-requirement artifacts in Rational Rose or ClearCase, nor any verification of such management.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: The test sets the precondition that the subscriber is registered, exercises establishing a trace, and asserts that a trace is established. This directly verifies the positive half of the requirement (registered subscribers are allowed to establish traces).

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: The test explicitly sets the precondition that the subscriber is not registered, attempts to establish a trace, and asserts as a postcondition that no trace is established. This directly verifies the negative half of the requirement (unregistered subscribers are not allowed to establish traces).

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test asserts that a subscriber registers with a subscriber manager (postcondition: subscriber is registered). It verifies a prerequisite (registration) but does not itself exercise or assert trace establishment or denial, so it does not directly verify the requirement.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test covers a subscriber failing to register and being removed. It addresses registration failure handling but does not assert behavior about establishing traces, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test concerns the subscriber manager registering with an event server and does not exercise subscriber trace-establishment behavior; it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: This test addresses behavior of a non-registered subscriber attempting to perform another action (establish a trace). It does not exercise or assert the registration action itself.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: The test explicitly exercises the registration action and asserts the postcondition that the subscriber is registered under the subscriber manager, directly satisfying the requirement's core behavior.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: This test addresses a negative path related to registration (aborting/failed registration) but does not assert successful registration. It is relevant to the registration workflow but does not fully verify the requirement's positive behavior.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: This test concerns the subscriber manager registering with an event server (manager-level registration), which is a different actor/registration purpose and does not verify a user registering as a subscriber.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: This test covers event publication and notifications to subscriber managers; it does not exercise or assert a user/subscriber registration action.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: TC143 explicitly instructs the subscriber to 'registers itself with the subscriber manager' and declares the postcondition that 'The subscriber is registered under the subscriber manager', directly matching the requirement's demanded behavior. This is direct, unambiguous coverage of the requirement's happy path.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: TC144 exercises the negative path where a subscriber does not complete registration. It references the act of not registering 'under a subscriber manager' (semantic relation) but does not assert the positive behaviour required (that on registration the subscriber registers under control of a subscriber manager). Thus it partially addresses the requirement by testing a failure mode but does not verify the required positive behavior.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: TC146 concerns the subscriber manager registering with an event server (IP, port, name) and does not assert subscriber registration under a subscriber manager.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: TC147 describes event publication/notification behavior and preconditions about subscriber manager availability; it does not assert subscriber registration under a subscriber manager.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: TC149 tests event notification reception and logging by a subscriber manager. Although it assumes 'the subscriber manager has registered subscribers' as a precondition, it does not assert or verify the registration action itself.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: TC141 explicitly exercises the core happy-path: a subscriber establishes a trace between a UML artifact and a requirement and the postcondition is that the trace is established. This directly maps to the requirement's intent to establish traces between artifacts and requirements. It does not, however, explicitly use the word 'subscription' nor demonstrate subscription directionality for other artifact types, therefore confidence is partial rather than full.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: TC142 covers a negative scenario relevant to establishing traces/subscriptions: it verifies that an unregistered subscriber cannot create the trace. This helps validate access/authorization constraints around trace/subscription creation, but does not prove the mechanics of a successful subscription from an artifact to a requirement beyond preventing it for unregistered actors.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: TC147 demonstrates that subscriptions to requirements exist and that subscribers receive notifications when requirements change. This supports the subscription concept in the requirement but does not show creating a subscription originating from an artifact (artifact-initiated subscription) or creation of a trace link itself.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: TC155 addresses refinement of a requirement and creation of links between requirements (requirement-to-requirement), not establishment of subscriptions/traces between artifacts and requirements. It therefore does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: TC162 is topically related (identifies requirements linked to the same artifact and notifies subscribers) but does not assert the creation of a subscription/trace from an artifact to a requirement. It describes event-message construction and notification, not trace/subscription establishment.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: The test explicitly exercises registration with a subscriber manager and states the postcondition that the subscriber is registered under the subscriber manager, which directly demonstrates an artifact (a subscriber) being placed under the control of a subscriber manager. Confidence is partial because the test covers a happy-path registration of one artifact type and does not demonstrate boundaries, error paths, or that every artifact type is covered.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test verifies that a subscriber manager registers with an event server (manager registers its IP/port/name). It does not assert that artifacts are placed under the control of a subscriber manager.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: The test indicates that a subscriber manager subscribes to requirements (precondition), which is topically related to control/management of requirement-linked artifacts, but it does not assert that artifacts are placed under the control of the subscriber manager or show a registration/assignment action for artifacts. Thus it provides weak, partial support.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: The test describes the event server identifying requirements linked to the same artifact and sending customized messages to identified subscribers. This shows linkage between artifacts and subscribers and communication to subscribers about artifact-related requirements, but it does not assert that artifacts are placed under the control of a subscriber manager (no registration/assignment action or enforcement is stated). Therefore it partially supports the requirement (covers linkage/notification aspects) but lacks explicit placement/control assertions and error/boundary behaviors.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test concerns processing event messages in order and prohibits out-of-order processing. It does not assert that artifacts are placed under the control of a subscriber manager or that any registration/assignment to a subscriber manager occurs.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: TC143 covers subscriber registration with a subscriber manager. It does not exercise deletion of subscriptions and therefore does not verify RQ107.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: TC144 is about a subscriber failing to register and removal from the system; it does not exercise or assert deletion of subscriptions, so it does not verify RQ107.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: The test case TC145 explicitly exercises the user deleting a subscription and asserts the postcondition 'The subscription has been deleted', which directly implements the requirement's stated capability.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: TC147 addresses event publication and notifications to subscriber managers; it does not perform or assert deletion of subscriptions and therefore does not verify RQ107.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: TC157 concerns deleting a requirement and related side effects (status set to obsolete, links tentative). It does not mention subscriptions or verifying that subscriptions can be deleted, so it does not verify RQ107.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: This test covers a subscriber registering with a subscriber manager (subscriber->manager), which is distinct from the manager registering with the eventserver (manager->eventserver). It does not provide evidence of the subscriber manager registering with the eventserver.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: This test explicitly performs the registration action: it instructs the subscriber manager to register its IP, port, and name with the event server and asserts the postcondition that the subscriber manager is registered with the event server. That directly satisfies the requirement's core action.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: This test checks receipt and storage of a non-speculative event notification but does not state that the subscriber manager performed registration with the eventserver. It therefore does not provide evidence for the manager registering itself with the eventserver.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: This test verifies that a subscriber manager receives and processes a speculative event notification, but it does not assert that the subscriber manager registers itself with the eventserver. The precondition mentions registered subscribers (subscribers registered under the manager), not registration of the manager with the eventserver. Therefore it does not cover the registration requirement.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: This test verifies that the event server can identify addresses and send notifications to subscriber managers and that subscriber managers receive event messages. It does not, however, assert that subscriber managers performed registration with the eventserver (identification might imply prior registration but is not an explicit assertion of the manager 'registering itself with the eventserver'). Thus it provides partial, indirect evidence toward the requirement but not an explicit registration verification.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: This test explicitly states a subscriber manager is online, labels the event as published in real time, and documents that an event notification is sent to the relevant subscriber manager, which directly exercises the push delivery when online.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: This test describes the subscriber manager coming online and downloading missed events (pull/retrieval after offline). It does not exercise or assert push delivery to an already-online subscriber manager in real time.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: The test shows an event notification being sent and received and stored, which aligns with push delivery to a receiving manager. However it lacks an explicit precondition that the manager is online and lacks explicit real-time timing/assertions and does not explicitly state the server-initiated 'push' action versus other delivery semantics.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: The test demonstrates sending and receipt of notifications (covers delivery behavior). It does not explicitly assert the manager is online as a precondition, nor does it contain explicit timing/real-time assertions or explicit statement that the server initiated a push connection.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: This test explicitly documents server-side identification of subscriber manager IP/port, building and sending a notification message to each subscriber manager and observes receipt, matching the server-initiated push delivery to subscriber managers (real-time notification flow).

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: TC146 tests registration with the event server (IP/port/name). Registration is a prerequisite but does not by itself verify that the subscriber manager pulls outstanding messages on first startup.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: TC147 addresses real-time publication to an online subscriber manager. It does not exercise the startup pull of previously stored messages when the subscriber manager first comes online.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: TC148 explicitly exercises the transition where the subscriber manager comes online with previously stored events on the event server and states as a postcondition that the subscriber manager 'has successfully downloaded the relevant event notifications from the event server', which directly implements the pull-on-startup behavior required by RQ110.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: TC149 describes receiving a non-speculative event notification and storing it locally; it concerns normal receive/store behavior while online and does not demonstrate pull-on-startup retrieval of outstanding events.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: TC150 covers receiving a speculative event notification while the subscriber manager is already registered/online. It does not exercise the 'first comes on line' pull behavior to retrieve outstanding stored events from the event server.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: The test asserts that an event notification is sent to the relevant subscriber manager (happy path). It does not explicitly assert that the subscriber manager receives or processes the notification (no receive/assertion/postcondition), so it only partially demonstrates the requirement.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: This test explicitly documents sending an event to the subscriber manager and that the manager receives and stores the notification, satisfying the requirement's reception obligation.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: The test explicitly states an event is sent to the subscriber manager for a registered subscriber and that the subscriber manager receives the event notification and processes it. This directly exercises the requirement's core behaviour: reception of notifications on behalf of managed artifacts.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: The test describes the event server sending notification messages to each relevant subscriber manager and states the postcondition that the event message is received by each relevant subscriber manager on behalf of its managed subscribers, directly matching the requirement.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: This test concerns ordering/display of events to a user and does not assert that a subscriber manager receives event notifications on behalf of artifacts it manages.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This positive test demonstrates that a registered subscriber can establish a trace between a UML artifact and a requirement, and that a trace is created. That maps directly to the 'manage traces' capability for a single artifact (creation/happy path). It does not show management across multiple artifacts, listing/querying traces, modifying or deleting traces, persistence across restarts, or concurrent/multi-entity behavior; thus only partial coverage.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This negative test asserts that when a non-registered subscriber attempts to create a trace, no trace is established. That behavior demonstrates enforcement of registration/access control for trace creation (a part of managing traces). It does not, however, demonstrate successful management operations (creation/storage/retrieval) for one or more artifacts, nor multi-artifact management, persistence, or lifecycle operations; therefore only partial coverage of the requirement.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test verifies that a subscriber can register with the subscriber manager (registration functionality). Registration is a prerequisite for managing traces but the test contains no assertions about trace creation, storage, retrieval, or multi-artifact trace management; it does not verify the requirement itself.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test verifies that the subscriber manager registers with an event server (network/registration behavior). It does not assert any trace management behavior, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test verifies receipt and logging of event notifications by the subscriber manager. While related to event handling, it contains no assertions regarding trace management (creation, storage, retrieval, or multi-artifact management), so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: Describes handling of a non-speculative event (stored in a local event log), which demonstrates a processing behavior distinct from the speculative handling in other tests. Alone it does not show explicit detection logic, but together with speculative tests indicates differentiation.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: Specifically addresses receipt and processing of a speculative event notification by the subscriber manager, indicating a distinct handling path for speculative events; however it does not explicitly assert detection vs. evolutionary events in the same test.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: Explicitly asserts the subscriber manager parses the incoming message to determine it is speculative and applies a distinct speculative processing flow. This demonstrates identification of speculative events but does not explicitly show identification of evolutionary events in the same test; therefore partial coverage of the differentiation requirement.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: This test describes the event server sending evolutionary events to subscriber managers but does not assert that the subscriber manager distinguishes evolutionary from speculative events or that it performs a different processing path upon receipt.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: This test focuses on the event server composing customized speculative event messages for subscribers; it does not assert subscriber manager behavior of differentiating speculative vs evolutionary events on receipt.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC146 concerns registration of the subscriber manager with the event server and does not assert that the subscriber manager stores evolutionary events in an eventlog.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC149 explicitly states that a non-speculative event notification (semantic match to 'evolutionary') is stored in the local event log, directly satisfying the requirement's core action and target (subscriber manager storing event in eventlog).

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC150 describes handling of speculative event notifications and processing protocols but does not assert storing speculative or evolutionary events into an eventlog. It does not provide evidence for the subscriber manager storing evolutionary events.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC159 states that the event server stores event details in its event log. This covers event server behavior, not the subscriber manager required by RQ114, so it does not verify the subscriber manager's storage responsibility.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC161 asserts that the event message is received by each relevant subscriber manager, but does not assert that the subscriber manager stores evolutionary (or non-speculative) event information in an eventlog. Reception alone does not satisfy the storage requirement.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC151 directly exercises the exact user action required by RQ115: it explicitly states the user constructs a speculative query using a GUI tool and expects the speculative event message to be emitted, satisfying the requirement's stated capability.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC152 focuses on subscriber manager processing of a speculative event after it has been issued; it assumes a speculative query exists but does not verify GUI-based construction of that query.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC155 is about refining requirements and associated events; it does not relate to constructing speculative queries via a GUI.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC156 addresses modifying requirements and modify events; it does not verify the GUI-based construction of speculative queries.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC162 covers the event server constructing composite event messages for a speculate event; it does not exercise or assert that a user constructed the speculative query via a GUI.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: TC149 addresses non-speculative event notifications and storing in a local event log; it does not pertain to speculative-event immediate processing.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: TC150 verifies that the subscriber manager receives a speculative event notification and that it is processed according to protocols. This covers the existence of processing behaviour for speculative events but does not assert immediacy (no timing/latency requirement verified) nor detail the processing actions, so it only partially supports the requirement.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: TC152 explicitly exercises the subscriber manager processing flow for a speculative event: receipt, identification as speculative, model injection, re-execution, restoration, and returning a response. This demonstrates the core processing behaviour required. However, the test text does not assert or measure 'immediacy' (timing/latency) nor specify boundaries or error conditions, so it does not fully verify the 'immediately' aspect of the requirement.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: TC162 exercises the event server constructing composite messages for a speculative event (server-side message construction), not the subscriber manager's immediate processing behaviour.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: TC164 concerns user attempts to process events out of order and the system prohibiting that; it does not assert processing of speculative events nor immediacy.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: This test concerns receipt/storage of non-speculative event notifications and does not address injection of speculative values or model re-execution.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: This test only verifies receipt/processing of a speculative event notification by the subscriber manager; it does not assert that the model is injected with speculative values or re-executed.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: This test covers user construction of a speculative query and the event server receiving a speculative event message; it does not verify subscriber manager behavior of re-executing a model with speculative values.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: The test explicitly states that the subscriber manager injects values (speculative values) into the performance model and re-executes it, then collates and returns results. This directly implements the requirement's action and expected behavior.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: This test concerns the event server constructing composite event messages and polling external systems; it does not assert that the subscriber manager injects speculative values or re-executes the model.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: This test covers event publication and that subscriber managers receive notifications; it contains no assertions about the subscriber manager reporting impact on other requirements/constraints.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: This test only asserts that the subscriber manager receives an event notification and processes it according to protocols. It does not assert that the subscriber manager analyzes/specifies whether speculative changes negatively impact other requirements/constraints.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: This test covers user construction of a speculative query and that a speculative event message is produced/received by the event server. It does not assert subscriber manager behavior about reporting negative impacts on other requirements/constraints.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: This test directly exercises the subscriber manager processing of a speculative event, model injection, re-execution, collation and return of results. Those steps align semantically with producing a report of impacts. However, the test text does not explicitly assert that the returned/collated results indicate whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints (no explicit assertion about detecting/reporting negative impacts or mapping results to other requirements/constraints). Therefore it partially covers the requirement.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: This test verifies that the event server constructs composite event messages including quantitative constraints from related requirements and sends them to subscribers. It exercises upstream message construction, not the subscriber manager reporting whether a speculative change negatively impacts other requirements/constraints. No assertion verifies the subscriber manager producing impact determinations.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC143 covers subscriber registration; it does not reference restoring model state.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC144 describes subscriber registration failure and removal; it does not address restoring an executable model's prior state.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC148 concerns coming online and downloading missed events; it does not mention restoring any executable or performance model state.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC149 addresses receipt and local storage of non-speculative event notifications; no restoration of an executable/performance model is described.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: The test case TC152 explicitly lists 'restores the model to its prior state' in the procedural steps and repeats the postcondition that 'The performance model is restored to its original state', directly asserting the requirement's behavior.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: Precondition asserts the trigger driver is running and post-condition checks an event message was sent, which semantically supports the requirement. Missing are explicit assertions that the driver implementation triggered the event and handling of driver failures.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: Test explicitly requires the trigger driver to be running and expects a decompose event message to be sent, which aligns with using a driver to trigger events. However the test is a high-level test description that assumes the driver precondition rather than asserting direct invocation/interaction with the driver or error/failure behaviors.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: Test text shows the trigger driver is expected to be running and that a refine event message is sent, supporting the driver-triggered events behavior. It does not verify driver invocation paths or error conditions.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: The test requires the trigger driver to be running and expects a modify event message to be sent, indicating events are produced. It does not demonstrate direct verification of the driver's role or error/edge behaviors.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: The test specifies the trigger driver running and expects a merge event message to be sent, which aligns with driver-triggered events. It lacks explicit checks that the driver component performed the trigger or coverage of failure modes.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.
Reason: TC153 explicitly names an 'Add New Requirement' test, steps include 'The user adds a new requirement' and the post-condition asserts a new event message is sent. This directly exercises the required event type and verifies the creation/send behavior required by RQ121.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.
Reason: TC155 documents adding an additional requirement as part of a 'refine' event. This demonstrates an event that results in adding a requirement, but it is scoped as a refine event rather than an explicit generic 'add new requirement' event; therefore it provides partial evidence for RQ121.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.
Reason: TC156 covers modifying an existing requirement ('The user selects an existing requirement and changes it') and post-conditions about modify events; it does not assert creation or addition of new requirements, so it does not verify RQ121.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.
Reason: TC157 describes deleting a requirement and setting its status to obsolete; it does not assert adding new requirements or an 'add' event, so it does not verify RQ121.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.
Reason: TC158 results in a 'newly merged requirement' as a post-condition, which implies creation of a new requirement entity. This provides partial evidence for the presence of an event that produces a new requirement, though it is a merge-specific event rather than a dedicated 'add' event.

Description: RQ122 Event types shall include decomposing one requirement into two or more requirements.
Reason: The test (TC154) explicitly exercises a decompose event, verifies that a single requirement is decomposed into two or more requirements/events, asserts a decompose event message is sent, and that temporary links are established. These statements directly realize the requirement's expected event type and behaviors.

Description: RQ122 Event types shall include decomposing one requirement into two or more requirements.
Reason: TC155 describes refining a requirement by adding related requirements (refine), which is a different event type from decomposing a single requirement into multiple requirements; it does not assert a decompose event.

Description: RQ122 Event types shall include decomposing one requirement into two or more requirements.
Reason: TC157 describes deleting a requirement and marking it obsolete; it does not exercise or assert a decompose event or splitting behavior.

Description: RQ122 Event types shall include decomposing one requirement into two or more requirements.
Reason: TC158 concerns merging two or more requirements into one and does not assert or exercise a decompose operation or splitting a single requirement into multiple requirements.

Description: RQ122 Event types shall include decomposing one requirement into two or more requirements.
Reason: TC162 concerns construction of composite event messages for a 'speculate' event and polling related requirements to build messages; it does not assert a decompose event or splitting a requirement into multiple requirements.

Description: RQ123 Event types shall include refining one requirement by adding an additional requirement to the existing one.
Reason: This test covers adding a new requirement (new event) but does not describe refining an existing requirement by adding an additional requirement related to it. No evidence of selecting an existing requirement and adding a related requirement.

Description: RQ123 Event types shall include refining one requirement by adding an additional requirement to the existing one.
Reason: This test describes a 'decompose' event (splitting a requirement into parts), which is a different operation than 'refine by adding an additional requirement'. No evidence of adding an additional related requirement to an existing one.

Description: RQ123 Event types shall include refining one requirement by adding an additional requirement to the existing one.
Reason: The test explicitly names a 'refine' event, requires selecting an existing requirement, and adding an additional related requirement. Post-conditions assert the requirement is refined (adds at least one related requirement) and describes messaging and temporary links, directly matching the requirement's expected behavior and related outcomes.

Description: RQ123 Event types shall include refining one requirement by adding an additional requirement to the existing one.
Reason: This test covers modifying an existing requirement (change), not specifically adding an additional related requirement to refine it. It does not assert the refine behavior required.

Description: RQ123 Event types shall include refining one requirement by adding an additional requirement to the existing one.
Reason: This test covers merging requirements into one, which is distinct from refining an existing requirement by adding an additional related requirement. No evidence of the required refine action.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.
Reason: TC153 covers adding a new requirement (new event), not modifying an existing requirement; it does not verify modify behavior.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.
Reason: TC154 covers a 'decompose' event, not a modify event; it does not assert or reference modifying an existing requirement.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.
Reason: TC155 covers a 'refine' event (adding related requirements), not modifying an existing requirement; no modify-event assertions present.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.
Reason: TC156 explicitly invokes a 'modify event', performs a change to an existing requirement, and lists post-conditions that the requirement is changed and a modify event message is sent. This directly demonstrates that a modify requirement event type exists and is exercised; the test also verifies side effects (message sent, links set tentative).

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.
Reason: TC157 covers a 'delete' event and setting status to obsolete, not a modify event; no evidence of modify behavior.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: This test covers adding a new requirement (a 'new' event). It does not reference deleting a requirement or a delete event.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: This test covers a 'decompose' event type; it does not reference deleting a requirement or a delete event.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: This test covers a 'refine' event type, not a 'delete' event. No steps or post-conditions mention deleting a requirement or a delete event.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: This test covers a 'modify' event type; it does not reference deleting a requirement or a delete event.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: The test explicitly names 'Delete a requirement' and contains steps that 'specifies a delete event' and 'selects an existing requirement and deletes it', plus post-conditions that a 'delete event message is sent' and the requirement becomes 'obsolete'. This directly verifies that a delete event type exists and its expected effects.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.
Reason: This test covers adding a new requirement (a 'new' event) and does not verify merge event types or merging behavior.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.
Reason: This test exercises a 'decompose' event (splitting requirements), which is not a merge and does not verify merging behavior.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.
Reason: This test covers a 'refine' event (adding related requirements) and contains no assertions about merging requirements or merge events.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.
Reason: The test explicitly exercises a 'merge' event, verifies that requirements are merged, that a merge event message is sent, and that links are reassigned—directly satisfying the requirement that event types include merging requirements.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.
Reason: This test concerns constructing composite messages for a 'speculate' event and does not assert or exercise a merge event or merging behavior.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test shows that when a requirement changes an event notification is sent (publish side). It does not assert that the eventserver receives that notification, so it only partially addresses the requirement.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test documents that adding a new requirement causes a new event message to be sent (emission). It does not verify that the eventserver receives that message, so it only partially covers the requirement.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test explicitly documents that when a requirement is modified a modify event message is sent. That covers the 'requirement changes' -> 'event message is emitted' part but does not show that the eventserver receives that message.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test directly verifies that the event server receives an event message and processes/stores it. However, it does not explicitly tie the received event to a requirement change trigger. Alone it does not prove the reception happens specifically 'when a requirement changes'.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: The test assumes the event server already has received a speculative event message and focuses on constructing a composite message for subscribers. It does not demonstrate that the eventserver receives messages specifically when a requirement changes nor the triggering relationship between a requirement change and reception.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.
Reason: This test asserts that a subscriber manager stores the received event notification in its local event log. That demonstrates the concept of recording an event in a log, but it is about the subscriber manager component, not the eventserver. Therefore it is related but does not fully verify the eventserver's obligation.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.
Reason: The test explicitly states that upon receiving an event the event server stores event details in its event log and the postcondition reiterates that the event is stored in the log. This directly satisfies the requirement's core behavior.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.
Reason: This test focuses on identifying impacted requirements and searching for subscribers after an event is received; it contains no assertion that the eventserver records the event in an event log.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.
Reason: This test covers building and sending notification messages to subscriber managers for evolutionary events; it does not assert that the eventserver records the event in its event log.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.
Reason: This test describes constructing and sending composite event messages for speculative events; it does not assert that the eventserver records the received event in an event log.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.
Reason: This test demonstrates that an event notification is sent to a 'relevant subscriber manager', which implies identification of relevant recipients, but it does not explicitly test that the event server identifies all relevant subscribers upon receipt (no explicit identification step or assertion that all subscribers were found).

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.
Reason: This test verifies that a subscriber manager receives and logs a notification for a registered subscriber. It does not exercise or assert that the event server identifies all relevant subscribers on receipt of the event.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.
Reason: This test explicitly exercises the identification steps (identifies impacted requirements; searches for subscribers) and states the postcondition 'All relevant subscribers are identified.' It demonstrates the core happy-path behavior but does not enumerate boundary/error scenarios, so confidence is partial.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.
Reason: This test assumes as a precondition that a list of subscribers has already been identified; it focuses on notifying identified subscribers rather than verifying that the event server performs the identification step upon receipt.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.
Reason: Like the previous test, this one treats identification as a precondition and then focuses on constructing composite messages for already-identified subscribers; it does not assert the identification action itself.

Description: RQ130 The eventserver shall differentiate between evolutionary and speculative events.
Reason: This test documents creation and receipt of a speculative event message but does not assert that the event server differentiates event types. It only indicates that speculative messages can be produced and received.

Description: RQ130 The eventserver shall differentiate between evolutionary and speculative events.
Reason: This test asserts that the subscriber manager (not the event server) determines that a message is speculative. It documents differentiation occurring in the subscriber manager, which is related but does not demonstrate the event server itself differentiates events.

Description: RQ130 The eventserver shall differentiate between evolutionary and speculative events.
Reason: The test explicitly states that the event server categorizes each event as speculative or evolutionary, directly matching the requirement's action to differentiate. This is a direct, unambiguous assertion of the required behavior.

Description: RQ130 The eventserver shall differentiate between evolutionary and speculative events.
Reason: This test exercises handling for an evolutionary event (notification to subscribers). It implies the system can identify evolutionary events, but it does not explicitly assert that the event server performs the differentiation step; it assumes an evolutionary event has been identified. Therefore it provides partial supporting evidence for differentiation (handling of evolutionary events) but not an explicit categorization assertion.

Description: RQ130 The eventserver shall differentiate between evolutionary and speculative events.
Reason: This test covers server behavior specific to speculative events (constructing and sending customized composite messages). It demonstrates handling of speculative events but, like the prior test, assumes the event is speculative rather than explicitly asserting that the event server performed the differentiation. Thus it partially supports the requirement by showing distinct handling per event type.

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.
Reason: This test demonstrates that when a requirement changes an event notification is sent to subscriber managers (happy-path notification). It does not explicitly label the change as an 'evolutionary event' nor assert that the eventserver performed all required identification steps or that notifications were received — so it partially covers the requirement.

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.
Reason: This test verifies that a subscriber manager receives a non-speculative (i.e., non-speculative could correspond to evolutionary) event notification and stores it. However, it does not explicitly assert that the eventserver initiated the notification upon occurrence of an evolutionary event (origin and triggering by eventserver are not asserted).

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.
Reason: This test verifies reception, parsing, categorization and logging, including identification of an event as evolutionary, but contains no assertion that the eventserver notifies subscribers. It is topically related but does not verify the notification requirement.

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.
Reason: This test explicitly contains an evolutionary event precondition, shows the event server identifying subscriber managers and endpoints, sending notification messages, and asserts the notification is received by subscriber managers — covering detection, identification, sending, and receipt.

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.
Reason: This test exercises construction and sending of composite messages, but is explicitly for a speculative event (not evolutionary). It therefore does not provide evidence that evolutionary events cause notifications to subscribers.

Description: RQ132 For speculative events; the eventserver shall construct a composite event message.
Reason: TC150 concerns the subscriber manager receiving an event notification and processing it. It does not assert that the event server constructs a composite event message.

Description: RQ132 For speculative events; the eventserver shall construct a composite event message.
Reason: TC151 describes a user constructing a speculative query and that a speculative event message is received by the event server. It does not assert that the event server constructs a composite event message.

Description: RQ132 For speculative events; the eventserver shall construct a composite event message.
Reason: TC152 focuses on subscriber manager processing of a speculative event (parsing the message, injecting values into a performance model, re-executing the model, and returning results). It does not assert that the event server constructs a composite event message.

Description: RQ132 For speculative events; the eventserver shall construct a composite event message.
Reason: TC159 verifies receiving, parsing, categorizing (speculative vs evolutionary), and logging of events by the event server. It does not assert construction of a composite event message for speculative events.

Description: RQ132 For speculative events; the eventserver shall construct a composite event message.
Reason: TC162 explicitly describes the event server constructing a composite event message for a speculative event and details the composition (changed requirement plus quantitative constraints) and sending the customized message to subscribers. This directly and fully satisfies the requirement's mandate.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test shows decomposition and linking of related requirements (relevant), but does not evidence the eventserver identifying all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.
Reason: The test documents creation of related requirements and temporary links, which is relevant to 'related requirements' but does not show the eventserver identifying all related requirements or obtaining constraints for impacted executable models.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test documents link reassignment when requirements are merged (relevant to maintaining related requirements), but does not show the eventserver identifying all related requirements or collecting constraints per impacted executable model.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test asserts that the event server identifies impacted requirements (core precursor) and finds subscribers, which partially addresses 'identify...requirements' but does not demonstrate identifying all related requirements or any constraints for each impacted executable model.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test explicitly states identification of all additional (related) requirements, retrieval of quantitative information (constraints) for those related requirements, and inclusion of those constraints in messages to subscribers — matching the requirement's elements (related requirements, constraints, per impacted recipient/model).

Description: RQ134 The eventserver shall retrieve quantitative values related to these additional requirements and constraints through querying the requirements management tool.
Reason: TC147 describes publishing an event in real time and notifying subscribers. It does not mention querying the requirements management tool for quantitative values or constraints.

Description: RQ134 The eventserver shall retrieve quantitative values related to these additional requirements and constraints through querying the requirements management tool.
Reason: TC153 covers adding a new requirement and sending an event message; it does not specify querying the requirements management tool or retrieving quantitative values/constraints.

Description: RQ134 The eventserver shall retrieve quantitative values related to these additional requirements and constraints through querying the requirements management tool.
Reason: TC154 covers decomposing a requirement and sending a decompose event with temporary links. It contains no step that queries a requirements management tool or retrieves quantitative values related to additional requirements.

Description: RQ134 The eventserver shall retrieve quantitative values related to these additional requirements and constraints through querying the requirements management tool.
Reason: TC155 describes refining a requirement and establishing temporary links and sending a refine event message. It does not mention querying a requirements management tool (DOORS) nor retrieving quantitative values or constraints.

Description: RQ134 The eventserver shall retrieve quantitative values related to these additional requirements and constraints through querying the requirements management tool.
Reason: TC162 explicitly states that the event server polls DOORS to obtain additional quantitative information and uses quantitative constraints from related requirements in a composite message, which directly exercises the core behavior of querying the requirements management tool for quantitative values. However, the test case text does not assert error paths, boundary conditions, authentication, conflict resolution of multiple quantitative constraints, or validation of retrieved values; therefore coverage of the full requirement is partial.

Description: RQ135 The eventserver shall create a composite speculative message which includes the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.
Reason: This test covers that the subscriber manager receives speculative event notifications and processes them; it does not provide evidence that the event server creates a composite speculative message with related requirements and constraints.

Description: RQ135 The eventserver shall create a composite speculative message which includes the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.
Reason: This test describes a user constructing a speculative query and the event server receiving a speculative event message, but it does not verify the event server constructs a composite message containing related requirements and constraint values.

Description: RQ135 The eventserver shall create a composite speculative message which includes the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.
Reason: This test concerns the subscriber manager processing a speculative event (parsing, injecting values, executing models). It does not assert that the event server creates a composite speculative message containing related requirements and constraint values.

Description: RQ135 The eventserver shall create a composite speculative message which includes the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.
Reason: This test verifies that the event server receives and categorizes events (speculative vs evolutionary) and stores them, but it does not assert that the event server constructs a composite speculative message including related requirements and constraint values.

Description: RQ135 The eventserver shall create a composite speculative message which includes the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.
Reason: This test explicitly states the event server constructs a composite event message that includes the changed requirement and quantitative constraints obtained from related requirements, which directly matches the requirement's mandate to include the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for impacted models. It also describes polling DOORS for quantitative information and sending customised messages to subscribers, covering the end-to-end behavior.

Description: RQ136 The eventserver shall send the customized message to each impacted executable model.
Reason: TC152 demonstrates the subscriber manager's handling of a received speculative event message (processing and responding). This shows that subscribers react to messages, but it does not demonstrate that the eventserver constructs or sends a customized message to each impacted executable model; thus it is only weakly related.

Description: RQ136 The eventserver shall send the customized message to each impacted executable model.
Reason: TC159 covers reception, parsing, categorization, and logging of incoming events but contains no assertion about constructing customized messages or sending messages to impacted executable models.

Description: RQ136 The eventserver shall send the customized message to each impacted executable model.
Reason: TC160 verifies identification of impacted subscribers (a necessary precursor to sending), but it does not assert construction of a customized message nor that the eventserver sends the customized message to each impacted executable model. Thus it partially supports the requirement (identification) but does not demonstrate sending or customization.

Description: RQ136 The eventserver shall send the customized message to each impacted executable model.
Reason: TC161 asserts that the event server sends a notification message to each relevant subscriber manager (proxy). However, it does not assert that the message is customized per impacted executable model nor that the server sends directly to each impacted executable model (it sends to subscriber managers). Therefore it partially covers the 'send' aspect but lacks explicit evidence of per-model customization and direct delivery to each executable model.

Description: RQ136 The eventserver shall send the customized message to each impacted executable model.
Reason: TC162 explicitly describes per-subscriber processing ('For each identified subscriber ...') and construction of a composite/customized message ('The event server constructs a composite event message ...') and asserts that this customized event message is sent to the relevant subscriber. This directly satisfies both the 'customized' and 'send to each impacted executable model' aspects of the requirement.

Description: RQ137 The user shall view all evolutionary events received for a specific artifact in the order in which they were received.
Reason: TC161 describes notifying subscribers when an event occurs (sending notifications) and does not assert any user view/display ordering for events received for a specific artifact.

Description: RQ137 The user shall view all evolutionary events received for a specific artifact in the order in which they were received.
Reason: TC162 concerns construction of composite event messages by the event server (speculative event handling and polling DOORS) and does not address user viewing or display ordering of events for a specific artifact.

Description: RQ137 The user shall view all evolutionary events received for a specific artifact in the order in which they were received.
Reason: TC163 explicitly states that event messages for a single artifact are displayed in the order received and requires multiple (>=3) messages in the precondition. This directly matches the requirement's intent to view evolutionary events for a specific artifact in received order, covering the core behavior.

Description: RQ137 The user shall view all evolutionary events received for a specific artifact in the order in which they were received.
Reason: TC164 enforces processing order (prevents processing out-of-order) and references multiple received messages for a single subscriber, which is related to ordering semantics, but it does not assert that events are displayed to the user in the order received or that all events are viewable. Thus it is weakly related to the requirement's display/view behavior and ordering.

Description: RQ137 The user shall view all evolutionary events received for a specific artifact in the order in which they were received.
Reason: TC165 confirms that event messages are displayed for a single artifact (matches the 'view ... for a specific artifact' portion) and exercises processing of the first message, but it does not assert that all events are shown nor that they are displayed in the order received. Therefore it provides partial topical support but not full verification of ordering/display completeness.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: TC147 verifies publication of an event in real time to subscribers; it contains no assertion that the user updates artifacts per message information.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: TC153 exercises adding a new requirement and sending an event message; it does not verify user updates to related artifacts following message contents.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: TC162 focuses on server-side construction of composite event messages; it does not assert the user updating related artifacts in response to messages.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: TC163 verifies ordering of displayed event messages but contains no step where the user updates artifacts based on message contents.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: TC165 directly exercises the user applying information from an event message to update an artifact and verifies postconditions that links are changed and related event messages removed. This maps to the requirement's user-driven update behavior and observable effects.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: TC157 covers deleting a requirement and setting existing links to tentative; it documents creation of tentative links but does not assert user removal of temporary traces or their cleanup from event processing/display.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: TC159 documents reception, parsing, categorization, and logging of events during initial processing but does not include any assertion about removing temporary traces or user-driven elimination of tentative artifacts.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: TC160 focuses on identifying subscribers for an event; it does not address elimination of temporary traces constructed by the eventserver.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: TC162 describes constructing composite event messages for speculative events (identifying related requirements, polling DOORS, composing and sending messages). It does not address user-driven elimination of temporary traces or removal of tentative links/messages.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: Test TC165 explicitly states the user selects tentative links to discard and that additional event messages based on eliminated tentative links are removed from the event processing list and display, directly matching the requirement's actor (user), action (eliminate unnecessary temporary traces), and effect (removal from processing/display).

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC149 verifies receipt and storage of event notifications in the local event log. It does not address automatic elimination of events when temporary traces are removed.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC155 asserts that temporary links are established when refining requirements. It does not assert that the system later automatically eliminates events when those temporary links are eliminated.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC157 describes deletion of a requirement leading to links set to tentative status and a delete event sent, but does not assert that events previously generated due to tentative links are automatically eliminated.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC164 describes prohibiting out-of-order processing and that no events are processed in that scenario. It does not assert that the system automatically eliminates events made invalid by user elimination of temporary traces.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC165 explicitly states that when the user discards tentative links (eliminates temporary traces), any additional event messages previously sent on basis of those tentative links are removed from the event processing list and display, which matches the requirement's automatic elimination of invalid events.
